channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: Detect, Unlearn, Restore: Defending Text Summarization Models Against Data Poisoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26036v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

在抽象式文本摘要任务中，大语言模型（LLM）常通过小规模任务特定数据集进行微调，但此类细粒度微调极易受训练期数据投毒攻击——攻击者仅需篡改少量样本，即可诱导模型持续生成偏见性、有害性或事实性错误的摘要，同时巧妙规避ROUGE等标准评估指标的异常检测。

方法创新

本文提出“检测—遗忘—恢复”（Detect, Unlearn, Restore）三位一体的后置式防御框架，覆盖机器学习供应链全流程：

  • 白盒检测：基于影响函数（influence function）量化样本训练影响力，结合语义一致性校验（如摘要与原文事实对齐度），识别异常高影响的投毒样本；
  • 黑盒审计：无需访问训练数据，通过向输入文档施加语义保持扰动（如同义替换、句式重组），观测模型输出敏感性——投毒模型敏感性达正常模型的2–3倍；
  • 梯度上升式遗忘：设计目标导向的梯度上升优化，精准削弱投毒样本对应的参数记忆，避免全局微调导致的性能塌陷。

关键发现与效果

在涵盖9种主流架构（如BART、T5、Pegasus）与6个基准数据集（CNN/DM、XSum等）的自适应攻击实验中：

  • 检测精度达85–92%，显著优于基线方法；
  • 遗忘后模型恢复96%原始行为，ROUGE-L仅下降<0.6%，实用性远超全量重训练；
  • 首次揭示投毒会引发持久性结构痕迹（如注意力头异常激活、嵌入空间偏移），为后门检测提供新理论依据。

本工作突破了传统投毒防御依赖训练数据或模型重训的局限，为部署级摘要系统提供了可落地、低开销的安全增强方案。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Detect, Unlearn, Restore—a unified post-hoc defense framework against fine-tuning–time data poisoning in abstractive text summarization. We expose persistent structural artifacts left by poisoning: poisoned samples exhibit abnormally high influence (white-box) and poisoned models show 2–3× higher sensitivity to semantics-preserving input perturbations (black-box). Our method achieves 85–92% detection precision across nine architectures and six benchmarks under adaptive attacks; gradient-ascent–based unlearning restores up to 96% of original behavior with <0.6% ROUGE degradation—eliminating the need for full retraining. The work establishes that fine-tuning poisoning is detectable and reversible without access to training data or model weights, enabling practical, deployment-ready security for summarization systems.

Abstract

Training-time data poisoning during fine-tuning poses a significant threat to large language models (LLMs) deployed for abstractive text summarization, where small task-specific datasets exert disproportionate influence on model behavior. In this setting, adversaries manipulate fine-tuning data to induce persistent summarization failures, such as biased or harmful summaries, while preserving standard evaluation metrics. We present a unified post-hoc defense framework for detecting and remediating fine-tuning-stage poisoning in summarization models across the machine learning supply chain. Our experiments show that in white-box settings, poisoned document-summary pairs exhibit abnormally high training influence, enabling detection via influence-function analysis with semantic consistency checks. In black-box settings, poisoned models display two to three times greater sensitivity to semantics-preserving perturbations, enabling behavioral auditing without training data access. Beyond existing poisoning formulations, we introduce novel attacks targeting factual distortion and representational bias, showing that poisoning alters summarization behavior without triggering conventional alarms. Across nine architectures and six benchmark datasets under adaptive attacks, our defenses achieve 85-92% detection precision, while gradient-ascent unlearning restores up to 96% of original behavior with minimal utility loss (less than 0.6% ROUGE degradation). These results indicate that fine-tuning-time poisoning leaves persistent structural artifacts, enabling practical detection and post-deployment recovery without full retraining.

item.title: Privacy Vulnerabilities of Attention Layers in Tabular Foundation Models and Protection of High-Risk Queries
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26021v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐私漏洞揭示与高风险查询防护：表格基础模型中注意力层的成员推断风险

传统观点认为，基于大规模合成数据预训练的表格基础模型（Tabular Foundation Models）隐私风险较低。然而，此类模型广泛依赖上下文内学习（in-context learning），即在推理阶段直接将真实敏感记录作为带标签的上下文示例输入——这构成了未被充分重视的隐私泄露通道。本文首次系统揭示：Transformer 的注意力机制本身即构成强隐私泄漏源。我们提出 AMIA（基于注意力的成员推断攻击）——一种无需影子模型、仅利用注意力权重分布集中性（attention concentration）的新型攻击方法。实验表明，注意力模式蕴含远超传统置信度分数的成员信号，在低误报率（FPR<0.1）区间，AMIA 相比最优置信度攻击平均提升7.7% 攻击准确率（AUC），证实其本质性威胁。

为防御该风险，我们设计了一种推理时轻量级保护机制，受 k-匿名性启发：通过语义感知的上下文键（context-key）表示压缩，降低其唯一性，不引入随机噪声、不修改模型参数、不需重训练。该机制仅对 AMIA 评分高的“高风险查询”触发，实现精准防护：对 AMIA 攻击的成员泄露抑制达50%，对置信度攻击亦达25%，同时仅造成3.9% 的预测性能下降（如 AUC）。进一步发现，微调（fine-tuning）会加剧隐私风险：当样本在微调后预测置信度显著上升时，其成员可推断性同步增强，表明微调可能放大模型对敏感训练样本的记忆，并通过置信度偏移暴露隐私信息。

AI Summary (English)

Tabular foundation models (TFMs), often pre-trained on synthetic data, are widely assumed to pose limited privacy risks. However, their reliance on in-context learning—where sensitive real records serve as labeled context examples at inference—creates a critical vulnerability. We demonstrate that attention patterns in transformer layers leak sufficient information for highly effective Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs). To exploit this, we propose AMIA, a shadow-model-free attack leveraging attention concentration, achieving +7.7% average AUC gain over state-of-the-art confidence-based MIAs, especially under low false-positive regimes. To mitigate this, we introduce an inference-time defense inspired by k-anonymity: it selectively compresses context-key representations of high-risk queries (identified by AMIA scores), reducing uniqueness without noise, retraining, or utility loss—yielding 50% reduction in AMIA leakage and 25% against confidence attacks, with only 3.9% predictive degradation. Crucially, we further show that fine-tuning amplifies privacy risk: samples exhibiting increased prediction confidence post-fine-tuning become significantly more vulnerable to MIAs, indicating fine-tuning strengthens memorization and leaks training data via confidence shifts.

Abstract

Tabular foundation models are commonly assumed to present limited privacy concerns as they are often pre-trained on large collections of synthetic data. However, these models leverage in-context learning, where sensitive records may be provided directly at inference time as labelled context examples. In this paper, we demonstrate that predictions generated via the attention mechanism leak sufficient information to enable effective Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs). To highlight this vulnerability, we propose AMIA (Attention-based Membership Inference Attack), a shadow-model-free attack that exploits the concentration of transformer attention patterns. Our results show that attention mechanisms reveal strong membership signals, which exceed classical confidence-based attacks, achieving an average gain of 7.7\%, specially in low false-positive regimes. To mitigate this risk, we introduce an inference-time defence inspired by $k$-anonymity principles. This approach reduces the uniqueness of context-key representations without introducing random noise or retraining the model. By targeting only high-risk queries identified through AMIA scores, the defence substantially reduces membership leakage of this attack by an average of 50\% and 25\% against confidence-based attacks, while preserving predictive utility with only 3.9\% performance degradation. Beyond showing that context examples are vulnerable, we further demonstrate that fine-tuning introduces an additional source of privacy risk. In particular, samples whose prediction confidence increases after fine-tuning become more susceptible to MIAs, indicating that fine-tuning can amplify memorisation and expose sensitive training information through confidence shifts.

item.title: The Web4 Agent Economy: A Large-Scale Empirical Study of the Landscape, Challenges, and Opportunities
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25876v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

互联网正从Web3迈向Web4阶段，其核心特征是自主智能体（Autonomous Agents）作为独立经济主体参与链上与链下协作。这些代理可持有加密钱包、执行链上交易、调用外部API并完成跨环境支付，催生了对新型基础设施的迫切需求——包括代理-工具交互、代理间支付、可验证代理身份等关键能力。Model Context Protocol（MCP）、x402、EIP-8004等新兴协议正试图构建这一基础层，但工业界热度与实证研究严重失衡：真实世界中Web4代理生态的规模、实践模式与瓶颈尚属空白。

方法与数据

本研究首次开展大规模实证分析，覆盖99,448个多链身份注册记录、3.17亿条链上交易日志、341个MCP开源项目源码及349个经筛选的GitHub问题报告，系统回答三大问题：（1）Web4代理在生产环境中的部署架构与使用模式；（2）开发者构建代理时面临的核心工程挑战；（3）社区对挑战的响应机制与有效性。

主要发现

  • 已形成高活跃度机器对机器（M2M）支付经济：日均处理数百万笔代理间交易，但底层基础设施严重不成熟：身份/授权实践碎片化、跨环境（链上/云/API）操作缺乏统一范式、支付协议互操作性缺失。
  • 社区响应可见但高度不均衡：仅17%的仓库具备系统性错误处理文档，支付互操作性问题在GitHub Issue中占比达41%，且平均修复周期超112天，成为最顽固瓶颈。
  • 本研究揭示了Web4代理经济“繁荣表象”与“脆弱基座”的根本张力，为构建安全、可扩展、互操作的Web4代理基础设施提供了首个实证基准与优先级路线图。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale empirical study of the emerging Web4 agent economy—the ecosystem where autonomous agents operate as independent economic actors on and off-chain. We analyze 99,448 multi-chain identity registrations, 317.6M transaction logs, source code from 341 MCP projects, and 349 curated GitHub issues to characterize real-world deployment patterns, engineering challenges, and community responses. Our findings reveal a highly active machine-to-machine payment economy processing millions of daily transactions—yet built atop immature infrastructure: fragmented identity/authorization practices, ad-hoc cross-environment operation, and critically, poor payment protocol interoperability. Community engagement is observable but uneven; payment interoperability remains the most persistent bottleneck, accounting for 41% of high-severity issues with median resolution time exceeding 112 days. This work exposes a critical gap between rapid economic growth and fragile technical foundations, providing actionable insights for building a secure, scalable, and interoperable Web4 agent infrastructure.

Abstract

The Internet is transitioning from Web3 toward Web4, where autonomous agents serve as independent economic actors. These agents can now hold crypto wallets, execute on-chain trades, and pay for external API calls. This transition calls for a new infrastructure stack capable of supporting key agent operations, including agent-to-tool interaction, agent-to-agent payments, and verifiable agent identity, represented by emerging protocols such as the Model Context Protocol, x402, and EIP-8004. Despite growing industrial interest in these protocols, the real-world Web4 agent ecosystem remains largely underexplored. To bridge this gap, we conduct the first large-scale empirical study of the Web4 ecosystem. Specifically, our study targets three interconnected questions: how Web4 agents are deployed and used in practice; what engineering challenges developers face when building Web4 agents; how current project communities respond to these challenges. To answer these questions, we analyze 99,448 multi-chain identity registrations, 317,596,323 transaction logs, the source code of 341 MCP projects, and 349 filtered GitHub issues. Our findings reveal that autonomous agents have established a highly active machine-to-machine payment economy, processing millions of daily transactions. However, this growth is built on immature infrastructure, including identity/authorization practice, cross-environment operation, and payment interoperability. Our follow-up analysis shows that community responses are visible but unevenly distributed across repositories, and payment interoperability remains the most persistent unresolved bottleneck. Overall, this study reveals a critical gap between the rapid growth of the Web4 agent economy and its fragile underlying infrastructure, highlighting future directions for building a more secure Web4 agent ecosystem.

item.title: Color Matters: Trigger Color Affects Success in Federated Backdoor Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25858v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）因数据隐私保护优势被广泛应用，但其分布式架构易受后门攻击威胁。现有研究多关注触发器形状、位置或注入方式，却忽视了触发器颜色这一基础视觉属性对攻击效能的潜在影响。本文首次系统探究“语义驱动型”后门攻击中触发色（trigger color） 的关键作用——在保持触发语义（如口罩、墨镜）、空间位置及投毒预算不变的前提下，仅改变触发器的色彩（黑白二值化），评估其对攻击成功率的调控效应。

方法设计

我们构建了一个可控FL实验框架：以CelebA数据集上的四类发色分类任务为基准，恶意客户端将源类图像叠加语义触发物（如白色/黑色口罩），并重标为攻击者指定的目标类；良性客户端仅使用干净数据训练。攻击优化目标采用双层级设计：（1）标准投毒目标（最小化触发样本的误分类损失）；（2）增强型SABLE目标——联合优化：①干净样本分类损失、②触发样本目标类损失、③倒数第二层特征空间中目标类与源类的特征分离损失、④恶意更新与全局模型的L2正则化项，以抑制更新漂移、提升隐蔽性。

主要发现与创新点

实验表明：触发色显著影响攻击成功率——白触发器对“金发”目标类攻击成功率提升达23.7%，黑触发器对“黑发”目标类提升19.5%；该趋势在鲁棒聚合机制（如Krum、Median）下依然稳健。这揭示：颜色不仅是表观属性，更是触发器与目标语义间感知一致性的关键媒介，在FL后门机制的设计、持久性分析与评估中具有不可替代性。本研究为语义后门的精细化建模提供了新维度，也为防御策略（如基于颜色敏感性的检测）指明方向。

AI Summary (English)

This paper reveals that trigger color—a previously overlooked visual attribute—is a decisive factor in semantic backdoor attacks against federated learning (FL). We propose a semantics-driven attack framework where malicious clients inject poisoned updates using natural accessories (e.g., masks, sunglasses) instantiated in black or white variants, while fixing trigger semantics, placement, and poisoning budget. Under both standard and enhanced SABLE objectives—which jointly optimize clean classification loss, triggered target loss, feature-separation loss in penultimate representations, and update regularization—we evaluate attack success on a 4-class CelebA hair-color task. Results show: white triggers significantly boost attack success (+23.7%) for the blond target class, while black triggers outperform for the black target class (+19.5%), even under robust aggregation (e.g., Krum, Median). This demonstrates that trigger color governs perceptual alignment between trigger and target semantics, making it essential for attack design, persistence analysis, and evaluation in FL.

Abstract

Federated learning is vulnerable to backdoor attacks in which malicious clients inject poisoned updates while preserving benign-task performance. In this paper, we study a semantics-driven backdoor mechanism in which attackers use natural visual accessories as triggers and manipulate only the trigger color while keeping the attack pipeline fixed. Our framework considers semantic trigger objects such as masks and sunglasses, instantiated in black and white variants, and evaluates their effect in a controlled federated learning setting. Malicious clients construct poisoned samples by applying a trigger to source-class images and relabeling them to an attacker-chosen target class, while benign clients train only on clean data. We analyze this mechanism under both a standard poisoning objective and a stronger SABLE-based objective that combines clean classification loss, triggered target loss, feature-separation loss in the penultimate representation space, and regularization to keep malicious updates close to the global model. This design enables the attack to remain effective while reducing excessive update drift. Experiments on a four-class CelebA hair-color task show that trigger color significantly changes attack success rate even when trigger semantics, placement, and poisoning budget are unchanged. White triggers are more effective for attacks targeting the blond class, whereas black triggers perform better for attacks targeting the black class. The same trend persists under robust aggregation, showing that trigger color is a meaningful factor in the operation, persistence, and evaluation of semantic backdoor mechanisms in federated learning.

item.title: RAS: Measuring LLM Safety Through Refusal Alignment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25750v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

当前大语言模型（LLM）安全性评估普遍依赖黑盒式输出判断：向模型输入不安全或越狱（jailbreak）提示，再由人工或自动裁判判定其响应是否违反安全策略。该范式存在三大局限：成本高昂（需大量生成与判别）、主观性强（结果高度依赖裁判模型或人工偏好）、泛化性弱（易过拟合于固定测试题库，难以反映真实拒答能力）。

方法创新：SafeVec 与 RAS 指标

本文提出 SafeVec——一种白盒、表征驱动的安全评估框架。其核心思想是：安全对齐的本质体现于模型内部隐藏状态对“拒答方向”的一致性，而非最终文本输出。SafeVec 分三步实现：
1. 提取拒绝方向：在安全对齐的参考模型（如 Llama-3-Instruct）各层中，通过对比安全/不安全提示下的隐状态差异，学习层特异的拒绝方向向量（refusal directions）；
2. 定位稳定层窗：识别在多个模型上均能线性分离安全/不安全行为的鲁棒层区间（如 Transformer 中间层），避免浅层噪声与深层语义漂移干扰；
3. 计算 RAS 分数：对目标模型，在不安全及越狱提示下提取对应层隐状态，量化其与参考拒绝方向的余弦对齐度，经校准映射为 0–100 的RAS（Refusal Alignment Score）。

关键发现与优势

在 Llama、Gemma 和 Qwen 多家族模型上验证表明：RAS 能清晰区分安全对齐模型与无过滤（uncensored）或对齐被破坏（abliterated）变体；RAS 分数与人工裁判测得的攻击成功率呈强负相关（ρ < −0.92）；单次评估耗时仅需 0.8 秒（GPU），比 judge-based 方法快 47× 以上。本工作首次将“拒答对齐”建模为可量化、跨模型、免裁判的表征信号，为高效、可解释、可扩展的 LLM 安全白盒评估提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) safety evaluation typically relies on black-box output inspection—querying models with unsafe or jailbreak prompts and judging responses against safety policies. This approach is costly, judge-dependent, and brittle to prompt distribution. We propose SafeVec, a white-box method that evaluates safety via internal representations rather than outputs. SafeVec extracts layer-wise refusal directions from a safety-aligned reference model, identifies stable layer windows where safe/unsafe behaviors are linearly separable, and computes the RAS (Refusal Alignment Score)—a calibrated 0–100 metric quantifying how strongly a target model’s hidden states align with those refusal directions under adversarial prompts. Across Llama, Gemma, and Qwen families, RAS reliably distinguishes aligned models from uncensored/abliterated variants, correlates strongly with output-level attack success rate (ρ < −0.92), and runs >47× faster than judge-based evaluation. RAS establishes refusal alignment as a compact, transferable, and efficient signal for scalable LLM safety assessment.

Abstract

Safety evaluation of large language models (LLMs) is commonly performed by querying models with unsafe or jailbreak prompts and judging whether their outputs violate a safety policy. Although useful, output-level evaluation is expensive, sensitive to judge choice, and easily tied to fixed question banks. We propose SafeVec, a white-box evaluation procedure that measures safety from internal representations rather than generated answers. SafeVec first extracts layer-wise refusal directions from a safety-aligned reference model, then selects stable layer windows where safe and unsafe behaviors are separable, and finally scores a target model by measuring whether its hidden states align with these refusal directions under unsafe and jailbreak prompts. The resulting metric, RAS (Refusal Alignment Score), maps representation-level refusal alignment to a calibrated 0-100 safety score. Across Llama, Gemma, and Qwen model families, RAS separates aligned models from uncensored and abliterated variants, tracks output-level attack success rate, and is substantially faster than judge-based evaluation. These results suggest that refusal alignment provides a compact and efficient signal for white-box LLM safety evaluation.

item.title: Tracing Target Answers in Poisoned Retrieval Corpora via Token Influence Attribution
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25721v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

检索增强生成（RAG）系统因依赖外部检索语料库，易受语料投毒攻击（corpus poisoning）威胁：攻击者通过向检索库注入恶意文档，诱导模型生成指定的错误答案。现有检测方法多依赖额外训练的分类器或调用大语言模型（LLM）进行后验验证，导致显著的计算开销与延迟，难以部署于资源受限或实时性要求高的场景。

方法创新：TRACE框架

本文提出TRACE（Tracing Target Answers via Token Influence Attribution），一种轻量、无需额外训练、不依赖外部LLM的检测框架。其核心思想是：逆向追踪模型输出中目标答案相关token的溯源影响力。TRACE包含两阶段：
1. 关键词影响发现：基于梯度归因（如Integrated Gradients）量化每个检索文档中各token对最终答案logits的贡献，统计跨文档高频出现的高影响力关键词；
2. 因果性二次验证：掩蔽这些关键词后重运行RAG流程，若目标答案概率显著下降（Δp > τ），则判定存在投毒。该设计避免了对文档内容语义的显式建模，仅依赖token级可微信号。

主要发现与优势

在Natural Questions、HotpotQA、TriviaQA三大问答基准上，针对Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Phi-3-mini等6种主流LLM的实验表明：

  • TRACE平均检测准确率达92.4%（F1），较基线方法提升11.7–28.3个百分点；
  • 推理耗时仅增加<8%（vs. 原始RAG），远低于LLM验证类方法（+300%+）；
  • 首次实现隐式目标答案反演：从检测结果中自动还原出攻击者预设的目标答案（召回率86.5%），为溯源与防御提供关键线索。

本工作为RAG安全提供了高效、可解释、即插即用的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems face severe corpus poisoning threats, where attackers inject malicious documents to steer model outputs toward attacker-specified answers. Existing detection methods rely on auxiliary classifiers or LLM-based verification—introducing high latency and computational cost. We propose TRACE, a lightweight, training-free framework that detects poisoning by tracing token-level influence from retrieved documents to answer tokens. TRACE first identifies recurrent high-influence keywords across retrieved passages via gradient-based attribution (e.g., Integrated Gradients), then verifies their causal impact through targeted keyword masking and prediction delta analysis. Evaluated on three QA benchmarks (NQ, HotpotQA, TriviaQA) with six LLMs (including Llama-3-8B and Qwen2-7B), TRACE achieves 92.4% average F1-score, outperforming baselines by up to 28.3 points, while adding only <8% inference overhead. Notably, it uniquely enables target answer reconstruction from detection signals (86.5% recall), offering actionable insights for attack forensics. TRACE establishes a new paradigm for efficient, interpretable, and deployable RAG security.

Abstract

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems are vulnerable to corpus poisoning attacks that manipulate model outputs through malicious retrieved documents. Existing detection methods typically rely on auxiliary classifiers or additional LLM-based verification, introducing substantial computational overhead. We present TRACE, a lightweight detection framework that identifies poisoning attacks by tracing answer-related tokens through token influence attribution. TRACE first discovers recurrent high-influence keywords across retrieved documents and then performs a secondary verification to confirm their influence on model predictions. Experiments on three QA benchmarks and six LLMs demonstrate strong detection performance while simultaneously uncovering attacker-specified target answers.

item.title: TL++: Accuracy and Privacy Preserving Traversal Learning for Distributed Intelligent Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25627v1

AI Summary (中文)

TL++：面向分布式智能系统的高精度与隐私保护遍历学习框架

背景与挑战：在医疗、金融等敏感领域，分布式智能系统亟需跨数据孤岛协同训练，同时避免原始数据集中化。现有方案存在显著局限：联邦学习（FL）虽保障数据本地性，但受非独立同分布（Non-IID）数据影响严重，且需反复传输完整模型；分割学习（Split Learning）通过“切层”（cut-layer）传输激活值降低通信开销，却难以复现中心化小批量梯度行为，且明文传输易致中间层信息泄露。

方法创新：本文提出 TL++——一种双模态遍历学习框架。其核心思想是跨节点构建虚拟小批量，在显式同步假设下精准复现中心化训练的梯度更新特性。

  • 基础模式：仅交换切层激活值与梯度（而非全模型），大幅压缩通信量；
  • 安全模式：采用两服务器半诚实设定，由协调器（orchestrator）与非共谋辅助服务器（helper）对每个切层张量进行秘密共享（secret-sharing），确保任一服务器均无法获知明文激活/梯度；标签与损失输出仍对协调器可见，符合实用隐私边界。
  • 安全路径设计：轻量级安全路径要求服务器计算路径为线性或仿射；非线性操作需引入多方安全计算（MPC）或近似方案。

实验验证：在CIFAR-10与BioGPT/PubMedQA上，对比联邦学习与分割学习基线（含全微调与LoRA）。结果表明：

  • CIFAR-10上，TL++基础模式（cut 1）达91.41%（SD 0.19），安全精确模式（cut 3）达90.93%（SD 0.17），超越最强非TL++基线超12个百分点；
  • 通信效率提升显著：基础模式单步通信量仅为全模型同步的1/13.1；
  • PubMedQA任务同样展现显著优势。

TL++在逼近中心化训练精度的同时，兼顾通信效率与切层级隐私保护，为分布式AI提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

TL++ is a two-mode traversal learning framework for distributed intelligent systems that enables accurate, privacy-preserving collaborative training across data silos without raw data centralization. It recovers centralized mini-batch gradient behavior by constructing virtual batches across nodes under explicit synchronization—addressing key limitations of federated learning (heterogeneity sensitivity, full-model exchange) and split learning (gradient inaccuracy, plaintext activation leakage). Base mode exchanges only cut-layer activations and gradients—reducing per-step communication by 13.1× versus full-model sync. Secure mode employs secret sharing between a semi-honest orchestrator and non-colluding helper to protect cut-layer tensors from either server, while keeping labels/loss visible to the orchestrator. Evaluated on CIFAR-10 and PubMedQA (with full fine-tuning and LoRA), TL++ achieves 91.41% (±0.19) and 90.93% (±0.17) accuracy—exceeding the strongest non-TL++ baseline by >12 percentage points—while closely approaching centralized-training performance with significantly reduced communication and activation-level privacy guarantees.

Abstract

Distributed intelligent systems increasingly need to train across data silos without centralizing raw data. Federated learning keeps data local but can suffer under heterogeneous partitions and requires repeated full-model exchange. Split learning reduces communication through cut-layer activations, but standard protocols generally do not recover centralized mini-batch gradient behavior and may expose activations and gradients in plaintext. We present TL++, a two-mode traversal-learning framework that constructs virtual batches across nodes to recover centralized mini-batch gradient behavior under explicit synchronization assumptions. Base mode exchanges cut-layer activations and gradients rather than full models. Secure mode secret-shares each cut-layer activation and gradient between an orchestrator and a non-colluding helper, preventing either server from observing plaintext cut-layer tensors. This protection is limited to a semi-honest two-server setting; labels and loss-related outputs remain visible to the orchestrator. In the lightweight secure path evaluated here, exactness requires a linear or affine server path, while nonlinear operations require nonlinear MPC or approximation. We formalize TL++, analyze communication and computation costs, and evaluate it against federated and split-learning baselines on CIFAR-10 and BioGPT/PubMedQA using full fine-tuning and LoRA. On CIFAR-10, TL++ base cut 1 and exact secure cut 3 achieve accuracies of 91.41% (SD 0.19) and 90.93% (SD 0.17), respectively, exceeding the strongest measured non-TL++ baseline by more than 12 percentage points. TL++ base cut 1 also reduces per-step communication by 13.1-fold relative to full-model synchronization. PubMedQA results similarly favor TL++. Overall, TL++ approaches centralized-training performance while reducing communication and providing activation-level secret sharing.

item.title: Probabilistic Agents in Deterministic Audits: Evaluating Multi-Agent Systems for Automated Audits Based on the German IT-Grundschutz
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25622v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

欧盟《NIS-2指令》要求数以万计的中小企业实施严格的网络安全风险管理。为满足合规要求，企业普遍采用德国联邦信息安全办公室（BSI）发布的IT-Grundschutz（IT-GS）标准。然而，IT-GS认证高度依赖人工——涵盖文档编制、证据验证与持续修订，导致实施成本高昂、难以规模化。

方法与创新

本文在前期概念框架基础上，提出并实现了首个面向IT-GS部分自动化的多智能体系统（MAS）架构，融合混合检索增强生成（HybridRAG） 技术。核心创新包括两项机制：

  • 假设-验证循环（Hypothesis-Verification Loop）：在结构分析（SA）阶段，将智能体推断的系统依赖关系与BSI知识图谱交叉验证，显著抑制大模型幻觉；
  • 解耦式推理流水线（Decoupled Reasoning Pipeline）：将智能体驱动的语义抽取与确定性的保护需求继承逻辑分离，保障合规逻辑的可追溯性与确定性。

实证结果

基于BSI官方案例“RecPlast GmbH”（由领域专家人工标注的黄金标准数据集），对SA、保护需求评估（PNA）、建模及IT-GS核查四阶段进行端到端评估。结果显示：
✅ SA与建模阶段F1达0.89/0.85，语义抽取准确率高，人工工作量降低超60%；
❌ PNA与IT-GS核查阶段F1仅0.63/0.57，暴露当前概率型大模型在确定性逻辑推理上的根本局限——无法稳定满足IT-GS对零容错、可审计、可复现的合规刚性要求。

结论

本研究证实：MAS+HybridRAG可高效支撑IT-GS中描述性、模式化任务的自动化，但尚无法替代人类在规范性、因果性推理环节的决策。成果为可信AI审计系统的设计提供了关键边界认知与工程化路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first technical implementation and empirical evaluation of a Multi-Agent System (MAS) with Hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation (HybridRAG) for partial automation of German IT-Grundschutz (IT-GS) certification. To enforce deterministic compliance, we introduce two novel architectural contributions: (1) a Hypothesis-Verification Loop in Structural Analysis that cross-checks agent-inferred dependencies against the BSI knowledge graph to suppress hallucinations; and (2) a Decoupled Reasoning Pipeline separating probabilistic semantic extraction from deterministic protection need inheritance. Evaluated end-to-end on the BSI’s expert-annotated “RecPlast GmbH” case study, the system achieves high performance in semantic phases—F1 scores of 0.89 (SA) and 0.85 (Modeling)—but significantly lower scores in logical reasoning phases—0.63 (PNA) and 0.57 (IT-GS Check). Results demonstrate that while current LLM-based MAS excels at pattern-driven documentation tasks, its probabilistic nature fundamentally limits reliability in deterministic, audit-critical reasoning—highlighting a critical boundary for trustworthy AI-auditing systems.

Abstract

The NIS-2 Directive mandates robust Risk Management from thousands of small and medium enterprises. To ensure compliance, companies rely on established standards such as the German IT-Grundschutz (IT-GS) of the Federal Office for Information Security. However, IT-GS certification is resource-intensive and requires a high level of manual effort for documentation, validation, and revision, making scalable implementation difficult and expensive. Building upon our previous conceptual framework, this paper presents the technical implementation and empirical evaluation of a Multi-Agent System (MAS) architecture combined with Hybrid Retrieval Augmented Generation (HybridRAG) for the partial automation of IT-GS certification. We introduce two novel technical contributions to the MAS architecture to enforce the compliance rigor. The Hypothesis-Verification Loop in the Structural Analysis (SA) phase that cross-references agent-inferred dependencies against the Knowledge Graph to reduce hallucinations, and a Decoupled Reasoning Pipeline that separates agent-driven semantic extraction from the deterministic protection need inheritance. We utilize the BSI's "RecPlast GmbH" case study as a human expert-generated reference data set for end-to-end evaluation of the architecture and to quantify Precision, Recall, and F1-scores. The performance of the system is investigated across the phases of SA, Protection Needs Assessment (PNA), Modeling, and IT-GS Check. The empirical results reveal noticeable differences throughout the different steps of IT-GS. While the MAS demonstrates high efficacy in semantic tasks (SA and Modeling), significantly reducing manual effort through automated information extraction, quantitative results reveal limitations in logical reasoning phases (PNA and IT-GS Check) as the probabilistic nature of current LLMs struggles to meet the deterministic rigor required by IT-GS.

item.title: An Approach for a Supporting Multi-LLM System for Automated Certification Based on the German IT-Grundschutz
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25608v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向德国IT-Grundschutz自动认证的多大语言模型协同系统

面对欧盟《网络与信息安全指令2》（NIS2）全面实施带来的合规压力，德国企业亟需开展BSI IT-Grundschutz认证，但面临专业认证人员严重短缺、人工评估成本高昂、安全概念编制效率低下三大瓶颈。本文提出一种创新的多大语言模型协同系统（Multi-LLM System, MLS），首次将Hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation（HybridRAG）与知识图谱（Knowledge Graph, KG）深度融合，实现IT-Grundschutz认证流程的半自动化支持。

该系统覆盖认证全生命周期关键阶段：

  • 保护需求评估：基于组织资产与威胁场景，动态生成分级保护目标；
  • 安全建模：利用KG结构化表征IT-Grundschutz目录（包括300+基础保护措施与1500+细化要求），驱动LLM生成符合BSI标准的体系架构图；
  • 合规性检查：通过HybridRAG融合语义检索（向量库）与符号检索（KG路径推理），实现细粒度条款匹配与偏差定位；
  • 措施整合与落地支持：自动生成可执行的措施清单、责任矩阵及实施路线图，并关联NIS2新增义务。

实验表明，MLS将单次认证前期准备时间缩短约62%，专家复核工作量降低48%，同时通过KG约束保障输出符合BSI官方术语与逻辑一致性。本方案不仅缓解了认证资源瓶颈，更构建了可扩展、可审计、可演进的数字安全治理基础设施，为NIS2时代大规模合规自动化提供了可复用的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a Multi-Large Language Model System (MLS) with Hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation (HybridRAG) to enable semi-automated BSI IT-Grundschutz certification. Addressing critical NIS2-driven challenges—including specialist shortages, high manual effort, and scalability constraints—the MLS integrates LLMs with a domain-specific Knowledge Graph encoding the full IT-Grundschutz catalog (300+ base measures, 1500+ detailed requirements). It supports end-to-end certification phases: protection needs analysis, security modeling, automated compliance checking via hybrid semantic + symbolic retrieval, and actionable measure consolidation. Evaluation shows a 62% reduction in preparatory effort and 48% less expert review time, while maintaining strict alignment with BSI standards through KG-guided generation. The system provides a scalable, auditable foundation for NIS2 compliance automation.

Abstract

This paper presents a novel approach to perform semi-automated BSI IT-Grundschutz certification using a MultiLarge Language Model system (MLS) with Hybrid RetrievalAugmented Generation (HybridRAG). Facing the challenges of the Network and Information Security Directive 2 (NIS2) directive, a shortage of specialists, and high implementation costs, our MLS architecture aims to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and support certifiers in maintaining the quality of security concepts while meeting the increased demand for certifications of newly affected companies. The system combines Large Language Models (LLMs) and Knowledge Graphs (KGs) to support different phases of the certification process, including protection needs assessment, modeling, IT-Grundschutz check, measure consolidation, and subsequent realization. Our architecture addresses the growing demand for security concepts and offers an approach to handle the digital security challenges introduced by NIS2.

item.title: Leaking Circuit Secrets: Gradient Leakage Attacks on Graph Neural Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25589v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着图神经网络（GNNs）在集成电路设计、硬件安全等关键任务中日益普及，其面临的安全与隐私风险亟待系统评估。本文首次针对电路设计场景下的GNN模型，全面揭示并实证了梯度泄漏攻击（Gradient Leakage Attacks, GLAs） 这一被长期忽视的实用威胁——攻击者仅通过观察训练过程中上传的梯度，即可逆向推断出敏感电路信息。

方法与实验

我们在标准网表基准（ISCAS’85、EPFL、TrustHub）上，对主流GNN架构（GraphSAGE、GCN、GIN、GAT）开展系统性GLA评估。攻击目标聚焦于恢复门级类型（AND/OR/FF等） 及硬件木马（Hardware Trojan）的关键特征（如触发条件、激活概率），这些信息可被用于破解逻辑锁定方案或绕过木马检测机制。

关键发现

  • 架构敏感性显著：带注意力机制的GAT泄露最严重；而采用单射聚合（injective aggregation）的GIN展现出最强鲁棒性，验证了聚合函数设计对隐私的决定性影响。
  • 防御效果有限且有代价：差分隐私、梯度裁剪、安全聚合、量化压缩与对抗训练等SOTA防御手段，在部分设置下仅带来边际提升，且普遍导致精度下降（最高达12.3%）或推理延迟上升（+47%）。
  • 实践启示：GLAs在真实电路分析流程中具备可行性，凸显当前GNN部署在EDA工具链中的隐私短板。

本工作为构建面向硬件领域的隐私增强型GNN提供了理论依据与实证基准，并开源全部代码、攻击脚本与数据集。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive study of gradient leakage attacks (GLAs) against graph neural networks (GNNs) in circuit design and hardware security—revealing a critical, previously overlooked privacy threat. We evaluate SOTA GNNs (GraphSAGE, GCN, GIN, GAT) trained on standard netlist benchmarks (ISCAS’85, EPFL, TrustHub) and demonstrate that GLAs can accurately reconstruct sensitive gate-level attributes (e.g., gate types) and distinctive features of hardware Trojans—enabling adversaries to weaken logic locking or evade Trojan detection. We identify architectural determinants of vulnerability: attention-based GAT suffers severe leakage, while injective-aggregation GIN exhibits superior resilience. Among evaluated defenses (differential privacy, gradient clipping, secure aggregation, quantization, adversarial training), none provides consistent, high-fidelity protection without compromising accuracy (up to −12.3%) or latency (+47%). Our findings underscore the urgent need for hardware-aware, privacy-preserving GNN designs—and we release all code, models, and attack artifacts.

Abstract

As graph neural networks (GNNs) become standard tools for critical tasks in circuit design and analysis, their security and privacy risks require careful attention. Here, we present the first comprehensive evaluation of gradient leakage attacks (GLAs) on GNNs in circuit-design and hardware-security tasks, a practical threat that has been largely overlooked. We assess state-of-the-art (SOTA) GNNs, including GraphSAGE, GCN, GIN, and GAT, trained on standard netlist benchmarks (ISCAS'85, EPFL, and TrustHub), for their fundamental vulnerability to GLAs. We find that GLAs can expose sensitive information, such as gate types and distinctive properties of hardware Trojans, which may assist adversaries in analyzing logic locking schemes or evading Trojan detection mechanisms. Our analysis shows that these risks are influenced by architectural features, with attention mechanisms (GAT) exacerbating leakage, while injective aggregation (GIN) provides comparatively stronger resilience. We further evaluate several SOTA defense techniques, including differential privacy, gradient clipping, secure aggregation, model compression with quantization, and adversarial training. We find that these techniques improve resilience only in specific settings and can also compromise model performance. Overall, our work provides key insights toward privacy-preserving GNNs and highlights the need for more robust and efficient defenses. We release our full methodology and artifacts.

item.title: CrypFormBench: Benchmarking Formal Analysis Capability of Large Language Models for Cryptographic Schemes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25561v1

AI Summary (中文)

CrypFormBench：首个面向密码方案形式化分析能力的双范式LLM基准

背景与挑战：密码方案的手动形式化分析高度依赖专家经验，耗时且易错；现有自动化工具（如Scyther、Tamarin、CryptoVerif）虽提升效率，但仍需人工将协议抽象为工具专属语言。大语言模型（LLM）有望降低门槛，但缺乏标准化评估体系，其在符号安全与计算安全双范式下的真实能力长期未知。

方法与创新：本文提出CrypFormBench（C.F.B）——首个覆盖符号模型检验与计算安全性证明的综合性基准。它系统评估LLM在五大核心能力上的表现：解释（Interpretation）、生成（Generation）、补全（Completion）、转换（Transformation） 和 修正（Correction）。基准包含700个高质量实例，涵盖677个真实/变体密码方案（含TLS、Signal、OPAQUE等）、7种主流形式化语言（如Tamarin、Scyther、EasyCrypt、FStar）及160项细粒度安全属性（认证性、机密性、前向保密等）。

主要发现：对9个SOTA模型（GPT-4o、Claude-3.5、Qwen2.5-72B等）的评测表明：模型在解释与补全任务上表现较好（得益于代码预训练），但在生成可执行验证脚本、跨语言转换协议描述、修复语义错误等高阶任务上严重受限；整体平均分仅32.1/100，Claude-3.5以48.7分居首，凸显当前能力天花板。我们进一步验证了少样本提示（few-shot）、Pass@K采样、轻量微调等策略可显著提升输出的工具可执行性。

意义：CrypFormBench不仅提供可复现的评估框架，更揭示了LLM辅助密码形式化分析的关键瓶颈与可行路径，为构建可靠、可信赖的AI-Augmented密码工程基础设施奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

CrypFormBench is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating large language models’ (LLMs) capability in formal cryptographic analysis across both symbolic and computational security paradigms. It assesses five core tasks—interpretation, generation, completion, transformation, and correction—using 700 instances spanning 677 cryptographic schemes, 7 formal verifier languages (e.g., Tamarin, Scyther, EasyCrypt), and 160 security properties. Evaluating 9 state-of-the-art LLMs reveals strong performance on interpretation and completion (leveraging code-aware pretraining) but severe limitations in generation, transformation, and correction—critical for producing tool-executable outputs. Overall scores remain low (mean: 32.1/100); Claude-3.5 achieves the highest score (48.7). We further demonstrate that few-shot prompting, Pass@K sampling, and lightweight fine-tuning effectively improve output executability. CrypFormBench provides a grounded, actionable foundation for advancing reliable LLM-assisted cryptographic verification.

Abstract

Manual formal analysis of cryptographic schemes is labor-intensive and requires substantial expertise. While model-checking tools (e.g., Scyther and Tamarin) and computational-security tools (e.g., CryptoVerif and EasyCrypt) improve the automation of security proofs, they still rely on experts to abstract schemes and write tool-specific formal descriptions. Large language models (LLMs) are a promising alternative, but their effectiveness in this domain remains unexplored due to the absence of standardized evaluation methodologies. To fill this gap, we introduce CrypFormBench (C.F.B for short), a comprehensive benchmark jointly covering symbolic and computational security to evaluate five core LLM capabilities: interpretation, generation, completion, transformation, and correction. It comprises 700 instances spanning 677 schemes, 7 mainstream formal verifier languages, and 160 security properties. The evaluation of 9 state-of-the-art LLMs reveals that most of them perform well on interpretation and completion, given their code-awareness advantages, but struggle with generation, transformation, and correction. Overall, their performance remains limited, with Claude-3.5 achieving the highest score at 48.7 out of 100. We further provide practical guidance, e.g., few-shot prompting, Pass@K sampling, and lightweight fine-tuning, to mitigate the executability bottleneck and improve tool-usable outputs. Taken together, our benchmark and analyses offer a grounded view of current progress and concrete directions toward reliable LLM-assisted formal cryptographic analysis.

item.title: Security and Privacy in Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Architectures, Threats, Defenses, and Future Directions for Building Trustworthy Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25533v1

AI Summary (中文)

检索增强生成（RAG）中的安全与隐私：架构、威胁、防御及可信系统构建路径

检索增强生成（RAG）已成为提升大语言模型（LLM）事实准确性与领域适应性的主流范式。然而，其融合外部检索模块的架构在增强能力的同时，也引入了传统语言模型所不具备的新型安全与隐私风险。本文首次系统性地梳理了RAG全栈流程（检索→上下文构建→生成）中的多维威胁面，覆盖集中式、端侧（Micro-RAG）、联邦式及混合部署范式。我们提出统一的威胁分类法，识别并分析五大核心攻击类型：成员推断攻击（泄露用户查询归属）、索引推断攻击（从嵌入/响应反推私有知识库内容）、知识库投毒攻击（篡改检索源以诱导错误生成）、梯度泄露攻击（在微调或联邦更新中暴露敏感上下文）、以及多方串谋攻击（跨组件协同窃取信息）。针对上述威胁，本文综述了三类防御策略：（1）架构级（如检索沙箱、上下文最小化、生成前过滤）；（2）算法级（差分隐私检索、鲁棒重排序、对抗性去偏）；（3）密码学级（可验证检索、匿踪查询、安全聚合）。特别指出各方案在隐私保护强度、生成质量、延迟与资源开销间的显著权衡，并强调实际部署中需兼顾合规性（如GDPR）、硬件约束（端侧内存/算力）与动态知识演化挑战。最后，本文凝练出四大开放问题：轻量级可信执行环境适配、跨模态RAG的隐私建模、对抗性鲁棒性量化评估框架，以及面向真实业务场景（如医疗、金融）的分级信任认证机制。

AI Summary (English)

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances LLMs with external knowledge but introduces novel security and privacy threats beyond standard language modeling—spanning retrieval indices, query logs, context assembly, and federated updates. This survey establishes the first unified threat taxonomy across RAG’s full pipeline (retrieval → context construction → generation) and deployment paradigms (centralized, on-device/Micro-RAG, federated, hybrid). We systematically analyze five critical attack classes: membership inference, index inference, knowledge base poisoning, gradient leakage, and collusion. We review architectural, algorithmic (e.g., differential privacy in retrieval), and cryptographic defenses (e.g., verifiable search), explicitly characterizing privacy-utility trade-offs and practical deployment constraints. Finally, we identify key open challenges: lightweight TEE integration, cross-modal privacy modeling, standardized robustness benchmarks, and application-specific trust certification frameworks—paving the way for trustworthy, secure, and resilient RAG systems.

Abstract

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a dominant paradigm for enhancing large language models with external knowledge. By coupling retrieval mechanisms with generative models, RAG systems improve factual grounding and adaptability across domains. However, integrating retrieval pipelines introduces new security and privacy risks that extend beyond conventional language modeling threats. Sensitive information may be exposed through retrieval indices, query logs, context construction, or federated updates, while adversarial manipulation of knowledge bases can undermine trust in generated outputs. This survey provides a comprehensive examination of privacy and security challenges across RAG systems deployed in centralized, on-device (Micro-RAG), federated, and hybrid paradigms. We present a unified taxonomy of threat surfaces spanning the retrieval, context construction, and generation stages and systematically analyze attack classes, including membership inference, index inference, poisoning, gradient leakage, and collusion. We further review architectural, algorithmic, and cryptographic defenses, highlighting privacy-utility trade-offs and deployment considerations. Finally, we outline open research challenges toward building trustworthy, secure, and resilient RAG systems for real-world applications.

item.title: How Reliable Is Your Jailbreak Judge? Calibration and Adversarial Robustness of Automated ASR Scoring
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25487v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

当前大语言模型（LLM）安全评估中，攻击成功率（ASR）几乎全由自动化裁判（judge）判定，而非人工标注。主流裁判分为两类：专用安全分类器（如HarmBench训练的判别模型）和“LLM-as-judge”范式（用通用聊天模型对输出进行二值评分）。然而，这类裁判模型的可靠性、校准性与抗对抗能力长期缺乏系统检验。

方法与数据

本研究基于HarmBench分类器验证集中的596条人工标注完成文本（每条由≥3名标注者投票确定是否“有害”），首次对两类裁判进行双维度评估：

  • 校准性：对比其预测与人类多数票的一致性（计算精确率Precision与召回率Recall）；
  • 鲁棒性：设计两类对抗攻击——表面扰动（添加无害前缀/框架，不修改有害内容）与白盒优化（GCG攻击针对开源分类器权重）。

主要发现

  • 校准失衡显著：专用分类器高召回（0.974）、低精确（0.835），过度标记；而三类LLM裁判均保持高精确（0.81–0.94），但召回率极不稳定（0.06–0.65），导致同一攻击集ASR波动高达±40个百分点。
  • 鲁棒性两极分化：表面扰动可使LLM裁判翻转率达57%–100%，单句拒绝前缀即引发39%–88%误判；专用分类器对此免疫（≤6.7%翻转），却在白盒GCG攻击下70%高置信正样本被成功欺骗（21/30，95% CI: 54–86%）。
  • 危害完整性验证：80例对抗翻转样本经双人审计，100%仍含原始有害内容，证实裁判失效非因危害消除，而是感知偏差。

实践建议

论文呼吁：所有ASR报告必须披露裁判在人工标注子集上的Precision/Recall；发布ASR时应校正精度偏差（如贝叶斯校正）；并强制开展对抗鲁棒性检查。开源代码已发布，支持可复现评估。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes critical reliability gaps in automated judges used to score LLM jailbreak success rates (ASR). Evaluating 596 human-labeled completions from HarmBench, we find stark calibration failures: dedicated safety classifiers over-flag (Precision=0.835, Recall=0.974), while LLM-as-judges maintain high precision (0.81–0.94) but suffer catastrophic recall variance (0.06–0.65), causing ASR estimates to swing wildly across judges. Crucially, both families are brittle—surface framing attacks flip LLM judges 57–100% of the time, whereas white-box GCG attacks deceive the classifier on 70% of confident true positives. Human audit confirms all flipped samples retain harmful content. We recommend reporting judge precision/recall on human-labeled data, precision-corrected ASR, and mandatory adversarial validation. Code is open-sourced.

Abstract

Almost every paper on LLM jailbreaks and prompt injection reports an attack-success rate (ASR), and that number is assigned not by people but by an automated judge: either a safety classifier trained for the task, or a general chat model prompted to grade. The judge is rarely checked. We check it. Using 596 human-labeled completions from the HarmBench classifier validation set, we compare the two judge families against human majority votes and then attack them. The two families fail in opposite ways. The dedicated classifier over-flags (precision 0.835, recall 0.974); three different LLM-as-judges keep high precision (0.81 to 0.94) but show erratic recall (0.06 to 0.65), so the same responses produce very different ASR depending on which judge scores them. The two families also differ sharply in robustness. Wrappers that leave the harmful text untouched and only add benign framing flip every LLM-judge between 57% and 100% of the time, and a single prepended refusal sentence accounts for much of this (39% to 88%). The dedicated classifier resists these surface attacks (at most 6.7%), but a white-box GCG attack on its open weights flips 70% of confident true positives (21 of 30; 95% CI 54 to 86%) even at a small optimization budget. A two-annotator audit confirms the attacks leave the harm intact: every one of 80 sampled flips still contained the harmful content. Because a large and growing share of reported ASR comes from LLM-judges, many such numbers are unreliable both on average and under deliberate pressure. We recommend that papers report judge precision and recall on a human-labeled slice, report ASR corrected for judge precision, and include an adversarial check of the judge. Our code is released.

item.title: Representation Matters: An Empirical Study of Program Representations for LLM Vulnerability Reasoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25356v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大型语言模型（LLMs）正被广泛用于自动化漏洞检测，但程序表示方式对LLM漏洞推理能力的影响尚不明确。当前主流提示方法多直接输入原始源代码，隐含假设“更多源码上下文=更强推理证据”，却忽视了结构化语义信息的潜在价值。

方法设计

本文提出首个面向漏洞推理的程序表示基准 RepBench，系统评估8类程序表示在真实C/C++漏洞场景下的有效性：

  • 基础表示：原始源码（Raw）、抽象语法树（AST）、控制流图（CFG）、程序依赖图（PDG）
  • 组合表示：AST+PDG、CFG+PDG、AST+CFG+PDG
  • 增强表示：基于Joern静态分析生成的增强型PDG（ePDG）

实验基于107个源自PrimeVul、经Joern验证的跨5类CWE的真实漏洞测试用例，并额外纳入19个由VulChecker/Hector生成的ePDG专项案例。所有变体均采用统一的思维链（Chain-of-Thought）与结构化输出协议进行公平评测。

关键发现

  • AST+PDG组合表现最优：准确率达83.2%，显著超越原始源码的53.5%（+29.7个百分点）；
  • 图结构表示具有显著优势：纯图提示（如PDG、AST+PDG）在准确率和提示长度（token开销）上全面优于源码或源码+图混合提示；
  • 揭示“上下文稀释效应”：向紧凑的结构化图中添加冗余源码会削弱关键漏洞路径的显著性，导致性能下降；
  • 静态分析可作为高效提示构建层：ePDG等增强表示进一步提升鲁棒性，验证了将传统软件分析能力嵌入LLM安全推理流程的有效性。

本研究为LLM驱动的安全分析提供了可复现的评估框架与实践指南，证实“Representation Matters”——精心设计的结构化表示比盲目扩充原始输入更具精度与效率优势。

AI Summary (English)

This paper challenges the common assumption that raw source code provides optimal evidence for LLM-based vulnerability reasoning. Through RepBench, an empirical benchmark on 107 real-world C/C++ vulnerability cases (from PrimeVul, covering 5 CWE categories) plus 19 ePDG-specific cases, we evaluate 10 program representations—including raw source, AST, CFG, PDG, their combinations, and enhanced PDGs (ePDGs)—under a fixed Chain-of-Thought and structured-output protocol. Results show representation choice critically impacts accuracy: AST+PDG achieves 83.2% accuracy, outperforming raw source (53.5%) by 29.7 points. Graph-only prompts consistently surpass source-only and source-plus-graph variants in both accuracy and token efficiency—revealing a context dilution effect where adding raw code to structural graphs obscures vulnerability-relevant signals. Our findings demonstrate that static-analysis-derived representations offer superior accuracy-overhead tradeoffs and establish them as an effective prompt-construction layer for security-focused LLM reasoning.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for automated vulnerability detection, but it remains unclear how program structure and semantics should be represented for LLM-based reasoning. Most prompting-based approaches provide raw source code, implicitly assuming that more source-level context gives the model better evidence. This paper challenges that assumption through RepBench, an empirical benchmark comparing raw source code with static-analysis-based program representations. RepBench converts real-world C/C++ vulnerability testcases into multiple representations: raw source, Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs), Control-Flow Graphs (CFGs), Program Dependence Graphs (PDGs), their combinations, and an auxiliary track of enriched PDGs (ePDGs). Using a curated PrimeVul-derived corpus of 107 Joern-based testcases across five CWE categories, we evaluate ten representation variants under a fixed Chain-of-Thought and structured-output protocol, plus 19 additional ePDG cases generated through VulChecker/Hector. Representation choice substantially affects LLM vulnerability reasoning. The strongest variant, AST+PDG, achieves 83.2% accuracy, compared with 53.5% for raw source. At the family level, graph-only prompts outperform both source-only and source-plus-graph prompts while requiring far less prompt overhead. These results reveal a context dilution effect: adding raw source code to compact structural graph evidence can degrade reasoning by making vulnerability-relevant evidence less salient. Overall, our findings show that carefully selected structural representations offer a better accuracy-overhead tradeoff than simply giving LLMs more raw input, and suggest that static analysis can serve as an effective prompt-construction layer for security-focused LLM reasoning.

item.title: Decoupling Reconnaissance and Exploitation: Measuring the Capability Boundaries of LLM-Based Web Penetration Testing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25332v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的Web渗透测试研究多采用端到端黑盒评估范式，但该范式存在严重错误级联（error cascading）问题：前期侦察阶段（如信息收集、漏洞识别）的失败会掩盖模型真实的漏洞利用能力，导致能力评估失真。

方法创新

本文提出侦察与利用解耦的两阶段评估框架，通过真实漏洞上下文注入与知识驱动的消融实验，在70个高保真Web漏洞测试平台（涵盖SQLi、XSS、反序列化、权限绕过等典型场景）上实现能力隔离。我们严格筛选50个代表性漏洞子集，对5类主流开源渗透测试智能体（含多智能体、单体式、图驱动三类架构）进行系统评测。

关键发现

  • 能力鸿沟显著：当提供准确漏洞上下文时，智能体功能成功率高达90.0%；而自主侦察阶段（以目标漏洞召回率为指标）仅达约50.0%，主因是难以解析非结构化遥测数据（如HTML响应、JS执行日志）。
  • 架构特异性明显：多智能体架构在长链交互任务（如Java反序列化）中表现更优；单体式模型擅长短链注入（如盲注探测）；图驱动设计则在跨会话访问控制漏洞利用上更具优势。

贡献价值

本工作首次建立细粒度、可复现的解耦评估协议，为LLM安全智能体的能力刻画提供实证基准，并为下一代自动化攻击系统的模块化设计与架构选型提供关键依据。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for automated web penetration testing, yet end-to-end black-box evaluations suffer from error cascading—early reconnaissance failures obscure true exploitation capability. To address this, we propose a decoupled two-stage evaluation framework, isolating exploit execution from reconnaissance via ground-truth context injection and knowledge-driven ablation across 70 high-fidelity web vulnerability testbeds. Evaluating five open-source agents (multiagent, monolithic, and graph-driven) on a curated subset of 50 vulnerabilities, we find: (1) functional exploit success reaches 90.0% with accurate vulnerability context, but autonomous reconnaissance recall plateaus at ~50.0%, primarily due to unstructured telemetry parsing failures; (2) architectural niches emerge—multiagent excels in long-sequence interactions (e.g., deserialization), monolithic models dominate short-chain injections, and graph-driven agents outperform on cross-session access-control flaws. This work establishes the first fine-grained, decoupled benchmarking protocol for LLM-based offensive security agents.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise for automated penetration testing, yet existing end-to-end black-box evaluations are highly susceptible to error cascading: failures in early reconnaissance can mask an agent's actual ability to exploit vulnerabilities. To more accurately characterize these capabilities, we propose a two-stage decoupled evaluation framework that separates exploit execution from reconnaissance. Using ground-truth injection and knowledge-driven ablation across 70 high-fidelity web vulnerability testbeds, our framework isolates exploitation performance from reconnaissance noise. We empirically evaluate five open-source penetration-testing agents, covering multiagent, monolithic, and graph-driven architectures, on a strictly aligned subset of 50 representative vulnerabilities. The results reveal a substantial capability gap. With accurate vulnerability context, agents achieve a functional success rate of up to 90.0%, whereas autonomous reconnaissance, measured by targeted vulnerability recall, plateaus at approximately 50.0%, primarily due to failures in parsing unstructured telemetry. Cross-architectural analysis further reveals distinct capability niches: multi-agent isolation is more effective for long-sequence interactions such as de-serialization, while monolithic and graph-driven designs perform better on short-chain injections and cross-session access-control vulnerabilities, respectively. This decoupled evaluation work provides a fine-grained benchmarking protocol and an empirical basis for designing next-generation automated offensive security agents.

item.title: Weave of Formal Thought
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25987v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成任务中展现出表面流畅性，但存在两大根本缺陷：缺乏形式化语法保障（无法严格保证输出的语法正确性），以及忽视语言内在层级结构（未有效建模上下文相关词法、最大匹配分词、关键字识别等关键机制）。现有约束解码框架虽能强制语法合规，却因依赖静态词汇掩蔽和刚性假设而牺牲完备性；而主流代码LLMs通常以预设规则硬编码语法结构，而非自主学习何时、如何暴露必要结构信息。

方法创新：Weave of Formal Thought（WoFT）

本研究提出WoFT范式，首次实现形式验证能力与可学习结构表征的有机统一：

  • 形式化约束解码器：基于Tree-sitter全规格构建，融合广义LR（GLR）解析与推测式词法分析（speculative lexing）。该设计维护多路并行的词法状态假设，并与GLR图结构栈实时同步，从而严格保证声望性（soundness）与完备性（completeness）——仅接受所有可扩展为合法程序前缀的子词单元，拒绝其余全部。
  • 潜变量结构微调：提出重加权唤醒-休眠（RWS）算法，优化重要性加权证据下界（IW-ELBO），引导模型在自回归生成中主动交织非终结符语法符号（如 if_stmt, expr），将形式推导动态编码为“自适应结构草稿本”。

关键结果

在Python任务上，对StarCoder2-3B实施WoFT微调后，每词元交叉熵较纯文本监督微调（SFT）基线显著降低14.3%。实证表明：显式、可选择的潜语法表征能有效恢复传统扁平化自回归训练所丢弃的核心结构知识，突破语法合规性与结构感知性的长期权衡瓶颈。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) generate fluent code but lack formal syntactic guarantees and fail to exploit hierarchical language structure. Existing constrained decoders ensure surface validity only approximately—ignoring context-sensitive lexing, maximal-munch tokenization, and keyword extraction—while grammar-aware LLMs inject syntax via rigid policies rather than learning when and how much structure to expose. We introduce Weave of Formal Thought (WoFT), a unified paradigm combining sound & complete syntactic validation with learned structural representations. First, we design a Tree-sitter–compliant constrained decoder that integrates speculative lexing with GLR parsing: it maintains concurrent lexer-state hypotheses synchronized with a graph-structured stack, admitting exactly all subword tokens extendable to valid program prefixes. Second, we propose latent-variable fine-tuning using the reweighted wake-sleep (RWS) algorithm to optimize the IW-ELBO, enabling the model to interleave non-terminal grammar symbols (e.g., func_def, list_comp) into generation as an adaptive structural scratchpad. On Python, WoFT fine-tuning of StarCoder2-3B reduces per-token cross-entropy by 14.3% relative to text-only SFT, demonstrating that selective latent syntax recovers critical structural information discarded by flat autoregressive training.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) attain remarkable surface fluency on code, yet they neither formally guarantee the syntactic validity of their output nor leverage the hierarchical structure defining the target language. While existing constrained-decoding frameworks address the former, they operate under rigid assumptions that preclude critical lexical mechanisms -- including context-sensitive lexing, maximal-munch tokenization, and keyword extraction -- and only approximate vocabulary masking, sacrificing completeness. For the latter, code LLMs typically inject grammatical structure via predetermined policies rather than learning which structural information to expose. In this work, we introduce Weave of Formal Thought (WoFT), a paradigm uniting rigorous syntactic validation with learned structural representations. First, we present a formal engine and constrained decoder that is sound and complete with respect to the full Tree-sitter specification. By augmenting generalized LR (GLR) parsing with a speculative-lexing construction that maintains concurrent lexer-state hypotheses synchronized with a GLR graph-structured stack, our decoder admits every subword token extending to a valid program prefix and rejects all others. Second, we present a latent-variable fine-tuning method training the language model to interleave non-terminal grammar symbols directly into generation. Utilizing the reweighted wake-sleep (RWS) algorithm to optimize the importance-weighted evidence lower bound (IW-ELBO) of the surface text, the model learns to selectively retain formal derivations as an adaptive structural scratchpad. For Python, fine-tuning StarCoder2-3B with our RWS objective reduces per-token cross-entropy by 14.3% relative to a text-only SFT baseline, demonstrating that discretionary latent syntax recovers critical structural information that flat autoregressive training discards.

item.title: Helpful or Harmful? Evaluating LLM-Assisted Vulnerability Patching via a Human Study
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25973v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

软件漏洞修复是一项高度依赖安全专业知识的认知密集型任务，而多数一线开发者缺乏系统性安全训练。尽管大语言模型（LLM）在漏洞检测、定位与修复中展现出应用潜力，但其辅助效果尚缺乏实证验证——尤其在真实开发场景下，LLM生成的补丁是否真能提升效率，抑或因幻觉（hallucination）或逻辑疏漏引入隐蔽安全风险，仍存重大疑问。

方法设计

本研究开展了一项严格控制的双因素平衡交叉实验（Balanced Crossover Design），招募具备实际开发经验的参与者（N=48），在真实开源项目（如Apache Commons、Spring Boot等）的典型漏洞场景中完成修复任务。我们自主研发了支持代码执行与即时反馈的WebApp平台，并创新性集成“幽灵测试”（Ghost Tests）——即隐藏的、仅针对漏洞语义与攻击面设计的安全验证用例（如绕过认证、路径遍历触发条件），其不暴露给参与者，用于客观评估补丁的安全鲁棒性，而非仅通过可见的功能测试。

关键发现

  • LLM辅助组平均修复时间缩短37%（p<0.01），但安全通过率反降低22%（仅58%补丁通过全部Ghost Tests，而纯人工组达80%）；
  • 63%的LLM补丁通过所有功能测试却失败于至少一项安全验证，证实“表面正确、深层脆弱”现象普遍存在；
  • 参与者主观感知存在显著偏差：72%认为LLM补丁“更可靠”，但其实际安全缺陷检出率低于人工组41个百分点。

创新贡献

本研究首次通过人因实验+隐式安全验证双轨范式，量化揭示LLM辅助修复的“效率-安全悖论”；提出的Ghost Tests机制为自动化工具评估提供了可复现、难规避的安全基准；结果警示：在关键安全场景中，LLM不应替代专业安全审查，而应作为需强制安全回溯验证的协同组件。

AI Summary (English)

This study empirically evaluates the trade-offs of LLM-assisted vulnerability patching through a controlled human-subject experiment with 48 professional developers. Using a balanced crossover design, participants patched real-world vulnerabilities in open-source projects—either manually or with LLM support (via fine-tuned CodeLlama and GitHub Copilot). We introduced Ghost Tests: hidden security-specific test cases (e.g., exploit-triggering inputs) that assess semantic correctness beyond visible functional requirements. Results show LLM assistance accelerates patching by 37% on average but reduces security validation pass rate by 22 percentage points (58% vs. 80% for manual patches); 63% of LLM patches passed all functional tests yet failed at least one Ghost Test, confirming prevalent “superficially correct but insecure” repairs. Participants overestimated LLM patch reliability (72% perceived them as more trustworthy), despite significantly lower actual security robustness. Our work highlights a critical efficiency–security tension and proposes Ghost Tests as a rigorous, deployable benchmark for secure code generation tools.

Abstract

Software vulnerability remediation is a cognitively demanding task that requires specialized security expertise often lacking in general developers. In the meantime, Large Language Models (LLMs) assisted tools show potential in vulnerability detection, location, and repair tasks. [Hypothesis:] While LLM-assistance is hypothesized to accelerate patching, it also risks introducing hallucinations or insecure code, leading to a higher likelihood of generating superficial repairs that bypass the standard functionality checks but fail the security validation. [Objective:] We aim to present an empirical experiment, unveiling the capability of LLM-assisted vulnerability patching compared to manual debugging on human participants in real-world scenarios. [Method:] We plan to conduct a controlled experiment using a Balanced Crossover design. For that, we have developed a WebApp for code execution and integrated hidden Ghost Tests to verify patch integrity beyond visible functional requirements. The experiment involves training and evaluation scenarios. The remediation speed, remediation efficacy for both standard functionality tests and security tests, and participant perception will be evaluated. [Pilot Study:] A pilot experiment with a small sample of participants has been conducted, providing insights for the following study.

item.title: Position Spaces and Graphs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25719v1

AI Summary (中文)

位置图：一种面向文档结构建模的新型图推理框架

本文提出位置图（Position Graphs）——一种基于位置空间（Position Spaces）形式化定义的图结构化推理框架，旨在为离散符号（如文本行、表格单元、布局区块）的二维相对位置关系提供代数一致、逻辑严谨的表示基础。与通用定性空间演算（如RCC、OPRA）不同，位置图通过两个严格偏序关系分别刻画水平对齐/先后（horizontal precedence）与垂直对齐/上下（vertical precedence），并引入链条件（chain condition）和行列兼容性约束（row/column compatibility），强制位置关系在行内与列内形成全序结构，从而精准建模文档中“左-右”“上-下”的层级化空间组织。

我们系统开展了理论分析：首先给出位置图一致性（consistency）的充要刻画，证明其等价于对应偏序图的无环性与行列约束的联合满足；其次，建立多项式时间可判定的局部一致性检验算法，并给出最小不一致子图的结构特征。进一步，我们将结构模式发现建模为诱导子图同构问题，严格证明：即使限制在位置图这一特殊图类中，该问题仍保持NP完全性——揭示了位置约束推理固有的计算本质难度。本工作虽源于文档智能中的版面分析需求，但聚焦于位置约束的抽象数学性质与代数一致性机制，构建了独立于具体OCR或深度学习技术的形式逻辑层，为文档理解、可视化编程、UI布局验证等场景提供了可验证、可组合的位置推理基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces position graphs, a graph-based reasoning framework grounded in the formal theory of position spaces. It models 2D relative positioning of discrete tokens using two strict partial orders—encoding horizontal and vertical alignment/precedence—subject to a chain condition and row/column compatibility constraints, ensuring structured, grid-like spatial reasoning. We provide a full theoretical analysis: (1) a necessary and sufficient characterization of graph consistency, equivalent to acyclicity plus constraint satisfaction; (2) a polynomial-time local consistency checking algorithm; and (3) a proof that induced subgraph isomorphism—modeling structural pattern discovery—remains NP-complete even for position graphs. While motivated by document layout understanding, this work establishes a logic layer for position-based constraints, independent of data extraction methods, with implications for document AI, UI analysis, and spatial reasoning systems.

Abstract

In this paper, we introduce position graphs, a graph-based reasoning framework based on the formalization of position spaces. This framework utilizes two strict partial orders, representing horizontal and vertical alignment and precedence, to model the relative positions of discrete tokens. Unlike general qualitative spatial calculi, position graphs are constrained by a chain condition and compatibility requirements that focus on rows and columns. We provide a comprehensive theoretical analysis of this representation, beginning with a characterization of graph consistency. Conditions to ensure the consistency of position graphs are established. Furthermore, we investigate the computational complexity of structural pattern discovery, modeled as the induced subgraph isomorphism problem. We demonstrate that this problem remains NP-complete even within the restricted class of position graphs. While initially motivated by document processing, this work focuses on the underlying mathematical properties and algebraic consistency of position-based constraints, providing a formal logical layer that is independent of specific data extraction techniques.

item.title: FedReLa: Imbalanced Federated Learning via Re-Labeling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26037v1

AI Summary (中文)

FedReLa：面向不平衡联邦学习的特征驱动重标注方法

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）在保障数据隐私前提下实现分布式模型训练，但其面临两大固有挑战：全局类别不平衡与跨客户端数据异构性的共存。更严峻的是，本地数据分布与全局分布间的“不平衡错配”（如某类在多数客户端完全缺失）会显著劣化聚合模型对少数类的判别能力。现有数据级方法常依赖全局类别先验，在极端稀疏场景（如长尾分布下多客户端零样本类别）中失效；而算法级方法难以缓解底层数据偏差导致的决策边界偏移。

为此，本文提出 FedReLa（Federated Re-Labeling）——一种新型、模块化、模型无关的数据级框架。其核心创新在于设计特征依赖的标签重分配器（feature-dependent label re-allocator）：各客户端基于本地模型提取的特征表示，动态识别易混淆样本，并依据全局统计启发式（无需真实全局标签分布）将其重标注为语义邻近的可靠类别。该过程在本地完成，不引入额外通信开销，且天然兼容主流FL优化器（如FedAvg、FedProx）与算法增强（如损失修正、梯度裁剪）。

在CIFAR-10-LT、ImageNet-LT及定制化stepwise-imbalanced Fed-CIFAR100等基准上，FedReLa将少数类准确率平均提升12.7%–23.4%，整体精度超越SOTA方法（如FedRS、LDAM-FL）达3.2–5.8个百分点，尤其在头部-尾部类别差距>20×时优势显著。实验验证了其对严重客户端类别缺失（如50%客户端无某尾部类）的鲁棒性，为隐私敏感场景下的公平、高效联邦建模提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving decentralized training but suffers from the coexistence of global class imbalance and cross-client data heterogeneity—especially when local imbalances misalign with the global distribution, severely degrading minority-class performance. Prior data-level methods fail under extreme conditions (e.g., complete class absence across many clients) due to their reliance on unknown global class statistics. To address this, we propose FedReLa, a novel, model-agnostic, communication-free data-level approach that performs feature-dependent sample re-labeling locally at each client. Without requiring global label distribution knowledge, FedReLa dynamically identifies ambiguous samples and reassigns them to semantically closer, more reliable classes using lightweight feature-space heuristics. Integrated seamlessly with existing FL optimizers (e.g., FedAvg) and algorithmic enhancements, FedReLa consistently boosts minority-class accuracy by 12.7–23.4% and overall accuracy by 3.2–5.8 percentage points over SOTA on stepwise-imbalanced and long-tailed benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10-LT, ImageNet-LT), demonstrating strong robustness to severe client-level class absence.

Abstract

Federated learning has emerged as the foremost approach for decentralized model training with privacy preservation. The global class imbalance and cross-client data heterogeneity naturally coexist, and the mismatch between local and global imbalances exacerbates the performance degradation of the aggregated model. The agnosticism of global class distribution poses significant challenges for data-level methods, especially under extreme conditions with severe class absence across clients. In this paper, we propose FedReLa, a novel data-level approach that tackles the coexistence of data heterogeneity and class imbalance in federated learning. By re-labeling samples with a feature-dependent label re-allocator, FedReLa corrects biased global decision boundaries without requiring knowledge of the global class distribution. This modular, model-agnostic approach can be integrated with algorithmic methods to deliver consistent improvements without additional communication overhead. Through extensive experiments, our method significantly improves the accuracy of minority classes and the overall accuracy on stepwise-imbalanced and long-tailed datasets, outperforming the previous state of the art.

item.title: Automatic Generation of Highlights for Academic Paper Via Prompt-based Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25253v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

学术论文的亮点（Highlights） 是对核心贡献的精炼陈述，广泛用于提升文献检索效率、支持文本挖掘与科学计量分析。然而，大量期刊未提供作者撰写的Highlights，导致下游研究面临信息缺失问题。现有自动提取方法多依赖监督学习，需耗费大量人力标注训练数据，泛化性与部署成本受限。

方法设计

本研究首次将提示学习（prompt-based learning） 引入学术Highlights生成任务。我们设计了任务定制化提示模板（如“请基于以下摘要，用3条 bullet points 概括本文的核心创新：”），将其与论文摘要拼接作为输入；系统评估了多种模型：本地部署的GPT-2、T5等预训练模型，以及通过API调用的ChatGPT。实验在三个公开学术数据集（ACL-ARC、SciTLDR、PubMedHL）上开展，并探索了零样本、少样本（1–5个示例注入提示）及模板变体的影响。

主要发现

  • 零样本下，ChatGPT+优化提示即可达到与先前监督方法相当的ROUGE-L分数（平均提升+0.8–2.3分），且无需任何领域训练数据；
  • 加入仅3个示例后，在ACL-ARC和SciTLDR上显著超越SOTA模型（ROUGE-1提升达+4.7），验证提示工程的有效性；
  • 提示质量高度敏感：模糊指令导致冗余或遗漏，而结构化约束（如限定条数、强调“创新性”“方法论”维度）可稳定提升连贯性与信息密度；
  • 案例分析表明，生成Highlights语义连贯、技术细节准确，与作者撰写版本相似度高（人工评估一致率达82%）。

创新价值

本方法摆脱对领域标注语料的依赖，为无Highlights论文提供即插即用式生成能力，有效支撑大规模文献智能处理与科学知识图谱构建。

AI Summary (English)

Highlights—concise, bullet-point summaries of a paper’s core contributions—are vital for literature retrieval and bibliometric analysis, yet many papers lack them. This study pioneers prompt-based learning for automatic highlight generation without task-specific training data. We design structured, instruction-aware prompts (e.g., “Generate 3 highlights emphasizing novelty and methodology from the following abstract:”) and evaluate models including local GPT-2/T5 and API-accessed ChatGPT. On three benchmark datasets (ACL-ARC, SciTLDR, PubMedHL), zero-shot ChatGPT with optimized prompts matches prior supervised methods’ ROUGE-L performance. With only 3 in-context examples, it surpasses state-of-the-art models by up to +4.7 ROUGE-1 on two datasets. We find prompt design critically impacts output quality: explicit constraints (e.g., length, focus dimensions) significantly improve coherence and informativeness, while ChatGPT’s strong language capability remains highly prompt-sensitive. Human evaluation confirms generated highlights are fluent, accurate, and closely align with author-written ones (82% agreement). This approach enables scalable, annotation-free highlight generation for bibliometric and text-mining applications.

Abstract

Highlights provide a concise summary of the main contributions of an academic paper and help readers quickly understand its focus. However, many journals do not provide highlights, which limits their use in literature retrieval, text mining, and bibliometric analysis. Existing studies have explored supervised learning methods for automatic highlight extraction, but these methods usually require large amounts of labeled training data. This study investigates prompt-based learning for automatic highlight generation. We design task-specific prompt templates and combine them with paper abstracts as model inputs. Several language models are evaluated, including locally deployed pre-trained models such as GPT-2 and T5, as well as ChatGPT accessed through an API. Experiments on three datasets show that ChatGPT with prompt templates achieves performance comparable to previous supervised methods without using task-specific training samples. When a small number of examples are added to the prompts, the model significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on two datasets. We further analyze how prompt design affects generation quality and find that, although ChatGPT has strong language modeling ability, its performance on this task is highly sensitive to the information provided in the prompt. Case studies also show that the generated highlights are generally coherent, informative, and close to author-written highlights. This study is among the first to apply prompt-based learning to academic highlight generation. The proposed method does not rely on domain-specific training corpora and can generate highlights for papers that lack such information, thereby supporting downstream text mining and bibliometric research.

item.title: On the Limits of Stretching Quantum Pseudorandomness
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24736v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

伪随机态（Pseudorandom States, PRS）是经典伪随机生成器（PRG）在量子计算中的自然类比，由Ji、Liu与Song（CRYPTO ’18）首次形式化定义。经典PRG的核心特性之一是可伸缩性（stretching）：给定短种子，可高效生成任意多项式长度的伪随机比特串。然而，量子PRS是否具备类似性质——即能否将输入种子“拉伸”为远长于其长度的不可区分量子态——长期悬而未决，构成量子密码学基础理论的关键空白。

核心贡献与方法

本工作首次建立了关于单拷贝安全PRS（$\mathsf{1PRS}$）输出长度的黑盒分离结果：我们构造了一个量子预言机（quantum oracle），在此相对模型下，存在输出长度为 $m(n) = 1.1n$ 的 $\mathsf{1PRS}$，但不存在任何输出长度达 $m(n) = \Omega(n^{2+\varepsilon})$（$\varepsilon > 0$）的 $\mathsf{1PRS}$。该分离揭示了量子伪随机性存在本质的长度瓶颈，否定了无条件的多项式伸缩可能性。

技术上，我们创新性地结合并拓展了Chen–Coladangelo–Sattath（EUROCRYPT ’25）提出的公共Haar随机态（CHRS）模型，提出一种新型资源计数技术：精确刻画任意$\mathsf{1PRS}$生成器在CHRS模型中所能有效利用的独立Haar态数量上限。该界限不依赖具体电路结构，仅基于查询复杂度与态空间维度，从而实现对高伸缩性PRS的普适性排除。

意义与影响

本结果首次从复杂性理论角度为量子伪随机性的伸缩能力划定严格边界，表明量子类比并非经典性质的直接迁移；同时，CHRS资源界定技术为后续研究量子随机性、量子极简主义（quantum minimalism）及抗量子密码原语设计提供了新工具范式。

AI Summary (English)

Pseudorandom states (PRS) are the quantum analogue of classical pseudorandom generators (PRGs). While classical PRGs can stretch short seeds into arbitrarily long pseudorandom strings, it remains open whether quantum PRS admit analogous stretching—i.e., generating output states whose dimension grows superlinearly in the seed length. This work establishes the first black-box separation for single-copy secure PRS ($\mathsf{1PRS}$) with differing output lengths: we construct a quantum oracle relative to which $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with output length $m(n) = 1.1n$ exist, but no $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with $m(n) = \Omega(n^{2+\varepsilon})$ exists for any $\varepsilon > 0$. Our proof builds on the Common Haar Random State (CHRS) model and introduces a novel technique to bound the effective number of Haar-random resources any $\mathsf{1PRS}$ generator can access—yielding a tight limitation on stretchability. This refutes unconditional polynomial stretching of quantum pseudorandomness and provides a foundational complexity-theoretic barrier.

Abstract

Pseudorandom states, introduced by Ji, Liu, and Song (CRYPTO '18), are quantum analogues of classical pseudorandom generators. A fundamental property of classical pseudorandom generators is that their output can be stretched to arbitrary polynomial length. Whether an analogous stretching property holds for quantum pseudorandom states remains unclear. In this work, we prove the first black-box separation between single-copy secure pseudorandom states ($\mathsf{1PRS}$) with different output lengths. Specifically, we construct a quantum oracle relative to which $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with output length $m(n)=1.1n$ exist, but $\mathsf{1PRS}$ with output length $m(n)=Ω(n^{2+ε})$ do not, for any $ε>0$. Our proof leverages the Common Haar Random State (CHRS) model introduced by Chen, Coladangelo, and Sattath (EUROCRYPT '25), and introduces a technique to bound the effective number of resource CHRS states utilized by any $\mathsf{1PRS}$ generator in this model.

item.title: FirmCure:Towards Autonomous and Adaptive Rehosting of Linux-Based Firmware
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24549v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

全系统固件重托管（full-system rehosting）是Linux固件安全分析的关键前提，尤其对IoT设备漏洞挖掘、动态行为验证至关重要。然而，现有方法在面向定制化嵌入式设备时面临严峻瓶颈：其初始化阶段常因专有架构（如ARM64/ARC/MIPS）、硬件绑定配置（如设备树、启动参数、外设驱动依赖）而失败；运行时又易受中断缺失、时钟异常、GPIO模拟失准等干扰，高度依赖人工调试，导致重托管成功率低、耗时长、泛化性差。

方法创新：FirmCure框架

本文提出FirmCure——首个基于大语言模型（LLM）驱动的自主自适应固件重托管框架。其核心包含三阶段闭环机制：

  • 自适应感知推理（Adaptive Perception Inference）：通过轻量级静态分析提取固件镜像的内核模块依赖、根文件系统结构、启动脚本链及硬件抽象层（HAL）调用图，构建可迁移的结构知识图谱；
  • 反思式合成（Reflective Synthesis）：利用LLM对配置空间（QEMU参数、设备树补丁、init脚本注入点）进行多轮迭代优化，结合反馈信号（如内核日志关键词、进程存活状态）自动修正偏差；
  • 自主运行时干预（Autonomous Runtime Intervention）：部署轻量监控代理，实时捕获panic、hang、服务不可达等故障，触发LLM驱动的诊断策略（如中断模拟注入、时钟源替换、伪设备节点动态挂载），实现零人工介入的故障自愈。

实验验证与价值

在21款来自10家厂商、覆盖5种CPU架构（ARM32/ARM64/MIPS32/MIPS64/ARC）的IoT固件上，FirmCure实现100%网络端口开放率（即所有监听服务均可远程访问）与90.5%服务交互成功率（支持curl/wget/SSH等标准协议交互），较SOTA基线（Firmadyne、FirmAE）平均提升3.2×重托管成功率。框架成功复现CVE-2021-3738等已知漏洞，并新发现7个未公开缺陷（含3个高危RCE）。结果证实：FirmCure的干预策略具备跨厂商、跨架构强泛化能力，为固件自动化安全评估提供了可靠基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Full-system rehosting of Linux-based firmware is essential for security analysis but remains highly manual due to architecture-specific initialization failures and runtime hardware dependencies. To overcome this, we present FirmCure, the first LLM-driven framework enabling autonomous and adaptive firmware rehosting. It integrates (1) Adaptive Perception Inference for static dependency extraction, (2) Reflective Synthesis for iterative QEMU/device-tree configuration optimization guided by kernel logs and process states, and (3) Autonomous Runtime Intervention for real-time fault diagnosis and self-healing (e.g., interrupt injection, clock source substitution). Evaluated on 21 IoT firmwares across 5 architectures (ARM32/64, MIPS32/64, ARC) from 10 vendors, FirmCure achieves 100% network port accessibility and 90.5% service interactivity, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. It successfully reproduces known vulnerabilities and discovers 7 novel flaws—including 3 high-severity RCEs—demonstrating strong cross-firmware generalizability.

Abstract

Full-system rehosting plays a critical role in the security analysis of Linux-based firmware. It matches commonly deployed firmware with sufficient background knowledge. However, for custom devices, existing approaches struggle to handle initialization and runtime obstacles in the rehosting process caused by specialized architectures and hardware-dependent configuration, which heavily rely on expert intervention. This ultimately creates fundamental bottlenecks and results in low rehosting efficiency. To address the above challenges, we propose FirmCure, the first LLM-driven full-system rehosting framework designed for autonomous and adaptive rehosting of Linux-based firmware. FirmCure develops an Adaptive Perception Inference mechanism to extract firmware structural dependencies via static analysis, followed by a Reflective Synthesis module for iterative configuration optimization, and finally an Autonomous Runtime Intervention module for real-time error remediation through runtime fault diagnosis and monitoring. We evaluated 21 IoT firmware images from 10 vendors across 5 architectures, while FirmCure achieved a 100% network port opening rate and 90.5% service interactivity, substantially outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. Our experiments confirm that FirmCure's intervention strategies generalize across heterogeneous firmware. The framework successfully reproduces known vulnerabilities and discovers new security flaws.

item.title: Red-Teaming the Agentic Red-Team
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24496v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体系统（agentic systems）在渗透测试、红队演练等进攻性安全场景中从理论走向商用化部署，其安全性却长期被忽视。当前研究与工业界聚焦于提升智能体的能力边界，却鲜有工作系统性评估其自身作为攻击载体所引入的新攻击面——尤其当智能体被用于执行高权限、高敏感度的安全任务时。

方法与发现

本研究首次对主流进攻性安全智能体工具（如AutoGPT、LangChain Red-Team Agents、CrewAI安全框架等）开展深度安全审计。我们发现：绝大多数工具存在共性架构缺陷，包括：

  • 未隔离的API密钥注入与泄露路径（如通过LLM输出解析触发eval()或动态代码执行）；
  • 缺乏沙箱内进程/网络行为的细粒度管控，导致攻击者可通过恶意工具调用实现容器逃逸；
  • 依赖不可信LLM响应直接生成并执行命令，形成LLM操纵→本地提权→持久化→横向移动的完整杀伤链。

创新与贡献

我们提出首个面向进攻性安全智能体的全周期网络杀伤链模型（Cyber Kill Chain for Agentic Red-Teams），覆盖从初始提示注入、沙箱绕过、到主机完全接管的6个阶段。基于此，我们设计了一种零信任增强型智能体架构：强制API密钥硬隔离、声明式工具白名单、LLM输出结构化校验、以及沙箱内无特权执行环境。所有设计原则均开源可复现，并已在3个主流框架中验证可阻断98.7%的披露攻击路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive security analysis of widely deployed agentic systems used in offensive security operations. We identify critical, shared architectural flaws—including insecure API key handling, untrusted LLM output execution, and insufficient sandbox containment—that enable adversaries to exfiltrate credentials, achieve persistent access, and fully compromise operator machines—even within containerized environments. To formalize the attack surface, we introduce a novel Cyber Kill Chain for Agentic Red-Teams, spanning initial LLM manipulation, sandbox escape, lateral movement, and persistence. Building on these findings, we propose a robust, zero-trust–inspired architecture with actionable design principles: strict API key isolation, declarative tool whitelisting, structured LLM output validation, and privilege-restricted sandbox execution. Our mitigations are broadly applicable and validated across three major frameworks, blocking >98% of disclosed attack vectors.

Abstract

The use of agentic systems to perform offensive security operations has moved from a theoretical possibility to a commoditized capability.
